Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Off Calendar

Defendant Budget Rent A Car System, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Hearing re: PLAINTIFF BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Defendant Budget Rent a Car System, Inc.'s motion to dismiss the third cause of action for intentional misrepresentation is denied.

CCP 581(f)(2) provides: "the court may dismiss the complaint as to that defendant when: (2) after a demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend, the plaintiff fails to amend it within the time allowed by the court and either party moves for dismissal." (emphasis added). Here, the court did not sustain a demurrer to the complaint; rather, it merely sustained a demurrer to a single cause of action.

In any event, even if movant was entitled to a dismissal, it would be without prejudice as it would be premature to enter any sort of "judgment." (CCP 577 ["A judgment is the final determination of the rights of the parties in an action or proceeding."].) While plaintiff currently cannot pursue the intentional misrepresentation cause of action, nothing would preclude plaintiff Emeruwa from seeking leave to amend to file an amended complaint re-pleading the claim if new facts were discovered. "Until entry of judgment, a ruling on a demurrer is not res judicata and may be reconsidered and changed by the trial court." (Valvo v. University of Southern California (1977) 67 Cal.App.3d 887, 892 n. 3.)

Budget's citation to McMillin v. Ventura Sav. & Loan Assn. (1971) 15 Cal.App.3d 588 is inapposite and actually undermines Budget's position. There, the court of appeal held that an interlocutory appeal from a "judgment" from a single cause of action was improper where other causes of action were still viable. The "judgment" was not final because other claims were still proceeding. Budget similarly is not entitled to a judgment or judgment of dismissal on the third cause of action. McMillin implicitly rejects Budget's position that the court can enter a judgment on a single cause of action. (Reply, 3:1-3.)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: C. Loke. (301/RCE)

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT (CONSOLIDATED CASE) GARRIN GOEBEL's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant GARRIN GOEBEL's ("Goebel") motion to strike is granted with 20 days leave to amend.

Judge Karnow previously sustained demurrers in favor of Goebel and plaintiff Emeruwa-Neuberg's sole cause of action against him is for negligence. Goebel seeks to strike the following prayers for relief as against him:

Paragraph 53 of Plaintiff's Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint (Page 10, line 9):
"attorneys' fees"

Portions of Plaintiff's prayer as follows: "2. For punitive damages for all appropriate defendants according to proof;" (Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint, Page 11, line 5)

Portions of Plaintiff's prayer as follows: "4. For reasonable attorneys' fees for all
appropriate defendants according to proof" (Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint, Page 11, line 7)

Portions of Plaintiff's prayer as follows: "5. Treble damages according to proof for all appropriate defendants" (Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint, Page 11, line 8).

Portions of Plaintiff's prayer as follows: "6. For statutory penalties according to proof" (Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint, Page 11, line 9).

These allegations are struck as irrelevant or improper as to Goebel because there is no present basis for the claimed relief. The cause of action against him is for negligence, there is no statutory or contractual basis for attorney's fees, and there is no underlying statute for penalties/treble damages. In other words, he is not an "appropriate" defendant. Plaintiff has leave to plead a claim (plaintiff suggests the Federal Debt Collection Practices Act) that would warrant the requested relief.

Order signed.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argumentadopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC. NOTICE OF APPLICATION OF WRIT OF POSSESSION AND HEARING Hearing

Plaintiff Budget Rent A Car System, Inc.'s application for a writ of possession is denied.

On August 7, 2023, Budget sought a writ of possession regarding a 2020 Kia that plaintiff allegedly wrongfully possesses. The court denied Budget's writ application on September 5, 2023. Budget now seeks another writ of possession over the same car in a nearly identical writ application filed October 10, 2023. This is a renewed motion under CCP 1008(b). Section 1008(b) requires a party bringing a renewed motion to submit an affidavit [or declaration] identifying new or different facts, circumstances, or law. The statute is jurisdictional. Even Zohar Construction & Remodeling, Inc. v. Bellaire Townhouses, LLC (2015) 61 Cal.4th 830, 840; D.R.S. Trading Co., Inc. v. Barnes (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 815, 821. See also, e.g., Randy's Trucking, Inc. v. Superior Court of Kern County (2023) 91 Cal.App.5th 818, 843. Budget has not complied with the statute's requirements. The court denies relief for non-compliance with CCP 1008.

Proposed order submitted for review.
